records.ety: [L. deformitas, fr. deformis: cf. OF. deformet, deformit, F. difformit. See Deform, v. & a., and cf. Disformity.]

defs: The state of being deformed; want of proper form or symmetry; any unnatural form or shape; distortion; irregularity of shape or features; ugliness.
quotes: To make an envious mountain on my back, Where sits deformity to mock my body.

defs: Anything that destroys beauty, grace, or propriety; irregularity; absurdity; gross deviation from order or the established laws of propriety; as, deformity in an edifice; deformity of character.
quotes: Confounded, that her Maker's eyes Should look so near upon her foul deformities.
